articles.title: Former Target Legal Chief’s Severance Package Exceeds $5 Million
articles.summary: Target Corp.'s former legal chief, Amy Tu, received $5.8 million in compensation in the year she was fired, including an initial severance payment of nearly $1 million. Her total severance package is valued at $5.3 million and will be paid out over two years. This compensation also included a pro-rated base salary and stock awards, following a $10.5 million pay package in the prior year.

articles.title: Former Target Legal Chief’s Severance Package Exceeds $5 Million
articles.summary: Target Corp.'s former legal chief, Amy Tu, received $5.8 million in compensation in the year she was fired. This includes a nearly $1 million initial severance payment, with a total severance package valued at $5.3 million to be paid over two years. Tu's 2025 compensation also encompassed a pro-rated base salary and $4.5 million in stock awards.

articles.title: Former Target Legal Chief’s Severance Package Exceeds $5 Million
articles.summary: Target's former legal chief, Amy Tu, received a severance package exceeding $5 million after her departure last spring. Her initial severance payment in 2025 was nearly $1 million, part of a larger plan to be paid over two years, bringing her total compensation for that year to $5.8 million, including salary and stock awards. This follows a $10.5 million pay package in 2024.

This follows a $10.5 million pay package in 2024.

articles.title: Former Target CEO Brian Cornell Scores $21.8M Pay Package in Final Year in the Corner Office
articles.summary: Former Target CEO Brian Cornell received a compensation package of $21.8 million in his final year, marking a 7% increase. Most of his pay came from stock awards, with his total compensation being scrutinized by shareholders who desire greater board independence. Shareholders will vote on a proposal for an independent board chair at the company's annual meeting in June.

articles.title: Former Target CEO Brian Cornell Scores $21.8M Pay Package in Final Year in the Corner Office
articles.summary: Former Target CEO Brian Cornell received a $21.8 million compensation package in his final year as CEO, a 7% increase from the previous year. Most of his pay came from stock awards, with salary, incentive pay, and bonuses making up the rest. Meanwhile, some shareholders are pushing for a more independent board structure, advocating for the board chair to be an independent director.

articles.title: [ARS] TARGET CORP SEC Filing
articles.summary: This article announces TARGET CORP's latest SEC filing, an ARS form, on April 27, 2026, at 04:20 PM. The filing has a low impact and neutral sentiment. The article also provides a brief overview of Target's stock data, including market cap, float, industry, and recent news and SEC filings.
